books.title: The Great Roxhythe
books.author: Georgette Heyer
books.summary: A Restoration-era courtier sacrifices his fortune, reputation, and chance at personal happiness to serve his king as a secret spy.

books.title: Ashenden
books.author: W. Somerset Maugham
books.summary: A collection of short stories based on the experiences of an intelligence agent during World War I.

books.title: The Education of Henry Adams
books.author: Henry Adams
books.summary: A prominent and caustic historian reflects on the reasons that “the more he was educated, the less he understood,” and what he believes the future holds for American society.

books.title: Queen Victoria
books.author: Lytton Strachey
books.summary: A sardonic and generally unflattering biography of Victoria Alexandrina, Queen of England.

books.title: Just William
books.author: Richmal Crompton
books.summary: An eleven-year old English boy gets involved in a continuous series of amusing adventures to the exasperation of his long-suffering family.

books.title: Manalive
books.author: G. K. Chesterton
books.summary: An eccentric man brings chaos and happiness to a house of litigious lodgers.

books.title: Man and Superman
books.author: George Bernard Shaw
books.summary: Jack Tanner flees both London and responsibility when he is made guardian of his childhood friend Ann Whitehead.

books.title: The Sketchbook of Geoffrey Crayon, Gent.
books.author: Washington Irving
books.summary: A collection of 34 essays and short stories, including “The Legend of Sleepy Hollow” and “Rip Van Winkle.”

books.title: The Portrait of a Lady
books.author: Henry James
books.summary: A young American woman travelling through Europe excites both admiration and intrigue.

books.title: The Beetle
books.author: Richard Marsh
books.summary: A group of middle-class Victorians find themselves pitted against an ancient Egyptian evil.

books.title: Seven Pillars of Wisdom
books.author: T. E. Lawrence
books.summary: T. E. Lawrence’s memoir of leading the Arab revolt against the Ottoman empire during World War I.

books.title: The Lodger
books.author: Marie Belloc Lowndes
books.summary: A maid and butler take in a mysterious, eccentric gentleman lodger around the time of the Jack the Ripper murders.

books.title: Red Dusk and the Morrow
books.author: Paul Dukes
books.summary: A newly-minted secret agent is sent to pick up the pieces of Britain’s spy networks after they are shattered by the Red Terror.

books.title: The Age of Reason
books.author: Thomas Paine
books.summary: Paine argues against the legitimacy of the Bible and institutional religion.

books.title: Poetry
books.author: Ameen Rihani
books.summary: A collection of Ameen Rihani’s poetry.

books.title: Point Counter Point
books.author: Aldous Huxley
books.summary: Several connected stories are interwoven to create a satirical look at English society.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: H. Beam Piper
books.summary: A collection of science fiction stories by H. Beam Piper ordered by date of publication.

books.title: Old Indian Legends
books.author: Zitkála-Šá
books.summary: A collection of Dakota legends retold by Zitkála-Šá.

books.title: The Varieties of Religious Experience
books.author: William James
books.summary: A philospher and psychologist surveys direct religious experiences, including healthy-mindedness, saintliness, conversion and mysticism.

books.title: The Cruise of the Alerte
books.author: E. F. Knight
books.summary: An English journalist sets sail in search of treasure buried on a remote desert island.
